brief_summary: Three-panel comparison of lube tolerance by housing. Nylon: wide amber forgiveness window. PC: narrower amber zone followed by a dashed bordeaux 'molasses' over-lube zone. POM: tiny amber zone — minimal lube budget. Each panel surfaces the working rule for that material.
data_sources: switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'How housings interact with lube' — 'Nylon is forgiving. It hides over-lubing well ... PC punishes over-lubing. The lower baseline friction means even a moderately heavy coat slows the switch noticeably ... POM barely needs lube on the rails ... Most POM modders lube only the stem legs'
switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'Why housing material matters' — 'The housing decides the budget and the margin for error.'
switch-housings-compared.mdx § 'Polycarbonate' — 'the fix is a thinner application, often a thinner oil like 105 on the rails'
